zh.Potterspury, Northamptonshire, England,: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾波特斯普利
zh.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry於大約1362年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾波特斯普利。她於1323年與Sir John de Paveley在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1413年在她的家鄉享年52歲。

zh-hans.Potterspury, Northamptonshire, England,: 英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾波特斯普利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry于大约1362年出生在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾波特斯普利。她于1323年与Sir John de Paveley在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1413年在她的家乡享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: Sturly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (North Yorkshire): nickname from Middle English st(o)urdi ‘bold, valiant; reckless, rash’ (Old French esto(u)rdi(t), past participle of estourdir ‘to daze or stupefy’).

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Sturry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: possibly a nickname from the Middle English adjective stere ‘hardy, strong’ and a range of related senses, with a further suffix -i; or perhaps from an adjective formed from stire ‘stire’, a word for two well-regarded types of cider apple once grown in the Welsh Marches.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry was born about 1362, in Potterspury, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir John de Paveley in 1323, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1413, in her hometown, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry est née environ 1362, à Potterspury, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Sir John de Paveley en 1323, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1413, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry wurde um 1362 in Potterspury, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1323 in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom Sir John de Paveley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1413 im Alter von 52 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry è nata il circa 1362 nel Potterspury, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Sir John de Paveley nel 1323, nel Glamorgan, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1413, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.Potterspury, Northamptonshire, England,: イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアポッテルスプウリー
ja.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturryは約1362年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアポッテルスプウリーで生まれた。1323年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンでSir John de Paveleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1413年に故郷で52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry(은)는 대략 1362년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 포터스푸리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1323년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 Sir John de Paveley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1413년 그녀의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Potterspury, Northamptonshire, England,: Potterspury, North Hamtunscire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry nasceu aproximadamente 1362, em Potterspury, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Sir John de Paveley em 1323, em Glamorgan, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1413, em sua cidade natal, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry родилась около 1362 г. в местности поттирспури, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Paveley в 1323 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1413 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Joan Sturly or Sturry nació aproximadamente 1362, en Potterspury, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Sir John de Paveley en 1323, en Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1413, en su pueblo, a la edad de 52 años.
